lesson.menu_desc: Meeting language and making suggestions -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 10 -- Meetings -- the UPAV Committee -- Making Suggestions (Let's / Why don't we / How about)
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Marcos Mansour | Aula 10 -- Meetings -- the UPAV Committee

listenings.text: Good afternoon, everyone, and welcome to the committee meeting. Let me start by going through the agenda. There are three items today. First, the report from last month. Why don't we begin there? Then we will move to the budget, and finally to the date of the next congress. Before we start, does anyone want to add an item? No? Good. Let's get started, then. On the first point, I would like to hear your views. Please feel free to comment. We have one hour, so let us keep it focused. Shall we go around the table?

Thank you, Chair. I would like to make a suggestion about the next congress. How about holding it in October instead of November? October works better for our international members, and the venue is cheaper at that time. We could also open registration earlier, so people have more time to plan. Of course, this is just a proposal. I am happy to hear other opinions. If the committee agrees, I can prepare a short plan for the next meeting. What do you all think? Shall we put it to a vote?
